verses.1: Job again took up his parable, and said,
verses.2: “Oh that I were as in the months of old,
verses.3: when his lamp shone on my head,
verses.4: as I was in my prime,
verses.5: when the Almighty was yet with me,
verses.6: when my steps were washed with butter,
verses.7: when I went out to the city gate,
verses.8: The young men saw me and hid themselves.
verses.10: The voice of the nobles was hushed,
verses.11: For when the ear heard me, then it blessed me,
verses.12: because I delivered the poor who cried,
verses.13: the blessing of him who was ready to perish came on me,
verses.14: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me.
verses.15: I was eyes to the blind,
verses.16: I was a father to the needy.
verses.17: I broke the jaws of the unrighteous
verses.18: Then I said, ‘I will die in my own house,
verses.19: My root is spread out to the waters.
verses.20: My glory is fresh in me.
verses.22: After my words they didn’t speak again.
verses.23: They waited for me as for the rain.
verses.24: I smiled on them when they had no confidence.
verses.25: I chose out their way, and sat as chief.
